message:
fix(events): bypass dedup when EventDeduplicator max_capacity=0 (ft-61kg4)

## Defect

crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:843 EventDeduplicator::check
with max_capacity=0 did NOT disable deduplication. The novel-key
eviction path at line 875 fired (entries.len()=0 >= 0=true), pop_front
on an empty insertion_order returned None, and the insert at line
881-888 still succeeded — producing a 1-slot ghost cache:

  check("a") → inserts "a". New.
  check("a") → finds "a" in entries. Duplicate.
  check("b") → evict "a", insert "b". New.
  check("a") → evict "b", insert "a". New. (!! should be Duplicate)

So alternating keys toggled "New" while immediate same-key repeats
spuriously returned Duplicate. That is neither of the two sensible
semantics for capacity=0 (strict "no dedup" = every call is New, or
strict "no storage" = always bypass).

Same attack/config shape as ft-bx4le in
connector_inbound_bridge::SignalDeduplicator (commit 8fdfd114, §1).

## Fix

Early-return DedupeVerdict::New at the top of check when
self.max_capacity == 0, before any entry-map bookkeeping. Zero
capacity now means "dedup disabled" — every call is New, entries
stays empty, no hidden state, no toggling.

## Regression

dedup_zero_capacity_bypasses_dedup_ft_61kg4 builds a deduplicator
with max_capacity=0 and asserts:
  - 5 immediate repeats of the same key all return New.
  - 3 cross-key calls (b, a, c) all return New.
  - After 8 check() calls, len() == 0 (no internal growth).
  - suppressed_count("a") == 0 (no shadow tracking).

The combination of "New on same key" and "len() == 0" is the pair
the ft-bx4le test pins too; it pins the difference between
"disabled dedup" and "stealth 1-slot cache" from the caller's side.

## Verification note

frankenterm-core currently cannot cargo test --lib due to pre-existing
blockers unrelated to this diff (ft-kqidb workflows/handlers missing
CompatRuntime, ft-uigpj DiagnosticOptions missing include_full_cwd,
ft-s2u51 asupersync dep graph split, ft-zv3u9 Event missing
PartialEq). The events.rs edit is purely additive — a short-circuit
at the top of check + a regression test in mod tests — so the logic
is textually verifiable and will exercise once any one of the four
blockers lands. No call sites changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
